After regional Fed presidents Kashkari and Hammack, two of the three dissenters from the April #FOMC decision who wanted to remove the "easing bias" from the Fed's statement, came out with hawkish speeches last week, the third of that group, Dallas Fed President Laurie Logan, in a speech in Texas Wednesday was the first to put a more specific timeline on when she needs to see inflation improving materially towards the 2% target saying "higher interest rates could be necessary later this year to fully restore price stability and appropriately balance both sides of the Fed’s dual mandate."

While a 2026 hike might be inferred from the wording used by others, such as  "in the near future," it is increasingly looking like we might be getting votes for hikes in the second half.

After noting that "economic activity remains strong," "consumer spending... robust," "corporate earnings [in aggregate] are going gangbusters," "financial conditions are accommodative... AI investment continues to boom," she says "conditions indicate that monetary policy is not restraining the economy."

BREAKING: The S&P 500 ETF, $VOO, has officially become the first ETF in history to surpass $1 trillion in assets under management (AUM).

$VOO has attracted +$69 billion in total inflows so far in 2026, on track for the largest annual intake since the fund’s inception in 2010.

This follows +$118 billion and +$138 billion recorded in 2024 and 2025, respectively.

Since the 2022 bear market, this fund’s AUM has more than TRIPLED.

By comparison, the AUM of S&P 500 ETFs, $IVV and $SPY, rose to ~$860 billion and ~$785 billion, respectively.

To put this into perspective, global ETF AUM stood at $21.9 trillion at the end of April, more than 3 times the $6.4 trillion held at the start of 2020, following 83 consecutive monthly inflows.

ETF investing has never been more popular.

trading is so much more about emotional regulation than anything or at least it's one of the biggest parts of it. and we all fall victim to it at times as well. I think you are in a good spot if a good or bad trade doesn't affect the way you treat others or really affect your personal life so much

a few other random thoughts, i do keep a ton of cash on hand at all time. likely more than i should which sometimes leaves me under-allocated but when things shit the bed it also gives me good opportunities to make a lot and return the cash. It is my defense, imagine playing soccer without a goalie, or hockey without defenseman. trading is a two way game. offense and defense. both need to be good. too many people are offense only and high stakes. but i also have a pretty complex portfolio setup by design which has it's pros and cons

you just have to accept that you are going to lose sometimes. its about being a good loser though, and knowing when you are wrong on something quickly

BREAKING: Venezuela’s crude oil exports have surged +61% YoY, to 1.25 million barrels per day, the highest in 7 years.

The surge was led by rising shipments to the US at ~558,000 barrels per day, India at ~427,000 barrels per day, and Europe at ~169,000 barrels per day.

In total, 67 cargoes were exported from Venezuelan ports in May.

Since November 2025, Venezuela’s oil exports have surged +150%, or ~750,000 barrels, 

This comes as the US eased sanctions on Venezuela's oil industry following the capture of Maduro earlier this year.

Venezuela's oil ministry is now targeting output of 1.37 million barrels per day by year-end, which would mark a +22% increase from 2025 levels and the highest since US sanctions were first imposed in 2019.

Venezuelan oil is increasingly flowing into global markets.

tweets.text:
8 illusions of Web3 builders / marketers / growth teams.

1. Registrations = users.

thread_tweets.text:
8 illusions of Web3 builders / marketers / growth teams.

1. Registrations = users.
thread_tweets.full_text:
8 illusions of Web3 builders / marketers / growth teams.

1. Registrations = users.

thread_tweets.text:
No. They are not.

Many Web3 builders still count registrations as users.
But in consumer products, a signup is only intent.

For neobanks for ex, the real user journey starts later:
account created → account funded → card activated → first transaction → repeat spend

Most GTM breaks between step 1 and step 3.
thread_tweets.full_text:
No. They are not.

Many Web3 builders still count registrations as users.
But in consumer products, a signup is only intent.

For neobanks for ex, the real user journey starts later:
account created → account funded → card activated → first transaction → repeat spend

Most GTM breaks between step 1 and step 3.

thread_tweets.text:
2. Acquisition is the bottleneck.

No, it's not.
A lot of Web3 consumer products do not have an acquisition problem.
They have an activation problem.

The campaign gets users to register.
The product fails to make them use it twice.
That is why “we have 500K users” often means very little without active usage data.
thread_tweets.full_text:
2. Acquisition is the bottleneck.

No, it's not.
A lot of Web3 consumer products do not have an acquisition problem.
They have an activation problem.

The campaign gets users to register.
The product fails to make them use it twice.
That is why “we have 500K users” often means very little without active usage data.

thread_tweets.text:
3. Designed for global markets = good.

No, it's not.

“Available in 150 countries” sounds good in a deck.
But broad availability is not the same as market penetration.

For Web3 consumer products, one strong local wedge can matter more than shallow global coverage.
thread_tweets.full_text:
3. Designed for global markets = good.

No, it's not.

“Available in 150 countries” sounds good in a deck.
But broad availability is not the same as market penetration.

For Web3 consumer products, one strong local wedge can matter more than shallow global coverage.

thread_tweets.text:
4. Referral programs protect safe UAC.

They don't. It's ez to game 90% of Web3 ref programs.

Most referral programs reward the wrong behavior.
They pay for signups.
But signups are cheap to fake. The better model is to reward activation, spend, repeat usage, or another behavior tied to actual product value.

KAST’s spend-linked referral design is a good example.
thread_tweets.full_text:

thread_tweets.text:
5. Transparency is optional

A huge illusion.
Public metrics are underrated as a GTM channel.
In Web3, traction updates are not only investor comms.
They become social proof.

In neobanks context: Spend volume, active cards, cashback paid, transaction growth, user milestones – all of this gives the market something to repeat.

Silence creates friction.

In Web3, traction updates are not only investor comms.

thread_tweets.text:
6. KOL content > user content.

They are two different things.

Example: Cashback screenshots.
Yeah, they look basic.
But for Web3 neobanks, they do an important job: they make the product believable.
A brand saying “earn rewards” is one thing.
Users publicly showing rewards is another.

thread_tweets.text:
7. Channels are the reason for poor conversion

No, they are not.
It's trust.

If your Web3 product touches money, trust is part of the product.
Not brand trust in the abstract.

Marketing cannot fix missing trust infrastructure.
thread_tweets.full_text:

No, they are not.

thread_tweets.text:
8. We onboard new users.

Wrong in 80% of cases. You make existing users switch.
So most of crypto products needs a switch trigger.
“Better UX” is rarely enough.

Users switch when the product gives them a clear reason: more yield / cashback / lower fees / social proof they trust

No switch trigger = no durable adoption.

tweets.text:
Token markets are broken by design.

Buying every new listing across the major CEXs since 2025 would have lost retail about half its capital. 

The median listing fell 82%, and exchanges have become a place where early holders sell into retail buyers.

Tokens with monthly unlock schedules underperformed BTC by about 7% in the week leading upto and weeks after each unlock event. 

What makes token outperformance difficult is that the next unlock usually arrived before the market has had the opportunity to absorbed the last.

Six major airdrops saw 78 to 94% of recipient wallets sell off most of their allocation by day 90. One of those programs paid roughly $1 billion in token value to wallets that haven't stayed around.

The best designs are simple. They now route revenue back to holders and tie supply releases to performance milestones. A revenue-weighted basket of the 10 highest-revenue protocols has outperformed BTC, ETH, and SOL since January 2025.

What's left is one of the strongest setups crypto investing has had: fewer tokens, better designed, and structural buyers replacing the hedge funds that exited.

tweets.text:
Noble Plains #Uranium @NOBLuranium $NOBL $NOBL.V just filed its NI 43-101 technical report for Duck Creek, and the story looks even better when you dig into the details.

✅ Maiden resource: 6.36M lbs U3O8 (5.32M lbs Indicated + 1.04M lbs Inferred)

✅ Resource exceeded its own Exploration Target by 30% on grade and 40% on GT

✅ Only ~2.5 miles drilled, while another ~2.75 miles of mineralized trend remains largely untested

✅ Additional Exploration Target already identified outside the current resource area

✅ Potential upside from the deeper Fort Union Formation not included in the resource

Most junior uranium companies would be thrilled to have a 6+ Mlbs resource. Noble Plains has that resource on roughly half the trend, with significant expansion potential still ahead.

What stands out is that the maiden resource didn't merely meet expectations — it outperformed the company's own target range. That's often a sign the geological model may be conservative.

Location matters too. Duck Creek sits in Wyoming's Powder River Basin, surrounded by projects owned by Cameco and UEC. In-situ recovery (ISR) deposits in established U.S. uranium districts continue to attract premium valuations as utilities and governments prioritize domestic supply.

2026 drilling now becomes the key catalyst. If management can convert the remaining Exploration Target into resources and demonstrate continuity along strike, today's 6.36M lbs could end up being just the beginning.

The market is valuing NOBL as a small explorer. The geology is increasingly suggesting something larger.

Disclosure: Paid partnership with Noble Plains Uranium. This is not financial advice. Please do your own due diligence.

That is slowly but surely changing. We are seeing the emergence of increasingly serious onchain businesses whose tokens genuinely capture and accrue the value created by the underlying network. At the same time, the market continues to separate the wheat from the chaff among existing projects.

The fundamentals for crypto have never been stronger. That is why we’re seeing more attention than ever from traditional finance and sophisticated investors.

The Golden Age is about to unfold as this trend continues. 

Tokens are dead, long live tokenization.

thread_tweets.text:
In 1999, Ray Kurzweil predicted human-level AI by 2029. Stanford's top AI experts said 100 years. Three years from the deadline, he says two things are still missing: AI that understands physics, and a robot that knows the difference between what goes in the fridge and what goes in the trash.

-- LLMs went from unremarkable to world-changing in 12 months. AI now outperforms human doctors by 50% in diagnosis.

 -- LLMs have only been truly effective for six months, according to Ray. A year ago, "not really all that impressive."

 -- The Genome Project was 1% done at the halfway mark. One doubling later — done. We're at that same moment with AGI. 

-- Software alone has improved roughly a millionfold over 70 years. Total compute gain = hardware (75,000 trillion fold) × software (million fold).

tweets.text:
$1.7B+ liquidated in 24 hours and around 290K traders wiped out.

That's what happens when everyone gets a little too comfortable with leverage.

Tthe liquidation number isn't the one I keep coming back to. Only ~24% of the top 100 coins are above their 50D trend, which tells me the market isn't healthy under the surface.

A few names might bounce, but most of the board is still sitting below trend.

Is this full macro panic? I don't think so. Traditional markets are holding up fine, so the pressure feels crypto-specific to me.

Leverage got way too crowded, and high beta is always the first thing people dump when they want to de-risk.

ETF demand cooling off into all of this didn't help either... the flows just aren't strong enough right now to say institutions are stepping in to absorb the dip.

So I'm playing it defensively for now. Until breadth picks back up and the ETF bid returns, I don't see a reason to get aggressive here.

It's a good time to be building watchlists though. The names you actually want are getting cheaper, and most people are too busy panicking to notice.

The best we can say looking at military conflicts in the past is that with the passage of time the stock market has tended to rise, and the more time that has passed, the more it has risen.

1. there's no "forever"
2. the four year cycle is hopium, even though it can drive self fulfilling flows
3. the btc underperformance trend vs AI related assets is very long, not noise
4. the eth underperformance trend vs almost everything is overwhelmingly evident
5. something going down when it should be going up it's signal (crashing solo is very different than crashing due to macro conditions)
6. I expect the AI trend to continue for a long time, and BTC to continue underperforming => there are much faster horses offering higher convexity than BTC
7. A handful of crypto assets can perform very well regardless.

For anyone with FOMO or thinking they should have known better: Don’t feel bad. 

No one has nailed the market this year, NO ONE. 

Not @LukeGromen. Not @jvisserlabs. And certainly not the permabears like @DowdEdward. 

They have all gotten it wrong in key aspects. 

AS HAVE I.

This has been a tough market to call right.

For example, Luke has basically been calling to not get “over your skies” on equities in general since the tariff debacle last year when the S&P was around 5,000. Up 50% since then. 

Jordi was advocating to sell semis like Micron around $600-700 and move into BTC and silver a month or so ago. He also said not to hope for new highs this year in the major indexes. Incorrect to be modest. 

Ed has been bearish forever.

As for me, I expected a BTC rebound. I nailed the bottom on equites and called for big buys on April 1, especially in semis, but way too early I said time to book those gains and rotate into gold, XLB, EWZ, and more traditional cyclical calls. 

BTW, I think that call I made early is probably starting to be right now.

At the end of day, I hope all have made money. I have. I’m sure Luke has with Gold as has Jordi with his AI plays and thematic baskets. But as for Ed, sorry bro. Give up the bearishness. It's really getting old. 

S&P will hit 10,000 by end of next year.
